title: ADD+git not working within buildah docker container
body:
### Issue Description

I'm running buildah as part of my gitlab CI to build docker images. This works fine in general, but ADD+git does not seem to work. I thought that support for that was added, so I wondered if it's something wrong on my end. Here's how I run stuff:

My `.gitlab-ci.yml` has the following buildah config: 
```gitlab-ci
.buildah_script:
  image: quay.io/buildah/stable
  variables:
    IMAGE_TAG: "latest"
    BUILDAH_FORMAT: docker
    STORAGE_DRIVER: vfs
  tags:
    - docker
  before_script:
    - echo "$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD" | buildah login -u "$CI_REGISTRY_USER" --password-stdin $CI_REGISTRY
  script:
    - cd "${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/${CI_JOB_NAME}"
    - buildah build --tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/${CI_JOB_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" .
    - buildah tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/${CI_JOB_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/${CI_JOB_NAME}:$(date -I)"
    - buildah push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/${CI_JOB_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
    - buildah push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/${CI_JOB_NAME}:$(date -I)"
```

This works just fine in general. However, when the Dockerfile I want to build has a 
`ADD https://<path>.git`, it will fail as in this example:
```
STEP 30/43: ADD https://${GIT_TOKEN_NAME}:${GIT_TOKEN_AUTH}@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#${GIT_BRANCH} target-folder
Error: building at STEP "ADD https://${GIT_TOKEN_NAME}:${GIT_TOKEN_AUTH}@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#${GIT_BRANCH} target-folder": reading "https://gitlab+deploy-token-<blah>@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#master": cloning "https://gitlab+deploy-token-25:<blah>@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#master" to "/var/tmp/3471609421":
: failed while performing `git init`: exec: "git": executable file not found in $PATH
```

Is this combination of running buildah in a Docker container and trying to use ADD+git not supported, or am I doing something wrong?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Try to build a Docker image via the buildah Docker image. 

### Describe the results you received

```
STEP 30/43: ADD https://${GIT_TOKEN_NAME}:${GIT_TOKEN_AUTH}@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#${GIT_BRANCH} target-folder
Error: building at STEP "ADD https://${GIT_TOKEN_NAME}:${GIT_TOKEN_AUTH}@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#${GIT_BRANCH} target-folder": reading "https://gitlab+deploy-token-<blah>@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#master": cloning "https://gitlab+deploy-token-25:<blah>@my-gilab-server.mydomain/project/something.git#master" to "/var/tmp/3471609421":
: failed while performing `git init`: exec: "git": executable file not found in $PATH
```

### Describe the results you expected

A successfull build. 

### buildah version output

```yaml
Whatever currently runs in the `buildah/stable:latest` image (currently `sha256:8136a3ab047c4112a2575908b319f6e661e4e4d6e99a88c5445c925b30de611e`):


[user@server]# docker run --rm --entrypoint /bin/bash -it quay.io/buildah/stable
[root@0b869638865f /]# buildah version
Version:         1.40.1
Go Version:      go1.24.3
Image Spec:      1.1.1
Runtime Spec:    1.2.1
CNI Spec:        1.1.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.35.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jun  5 14:13:27 2025
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
Executing `buildah info` in the container just ends with:


[root@0b869638865f /]# buildah info
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
ERRO[0000] parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax 
ERRO[0000] (Unable to determine exit status)
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
I don't think I know what this file is.
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

gitlab with CI running Docker container.

### Additional information

Building images that do not use ADD+git works just fine. I'm happy to learn what I do wrong or that this is simply not supported. Perhaps there are other Docker images than `buildah/stable` which include a `git` runtime? https://quay.io/organization/buildah didn't look like there would be more options...

comments.body: I think the image has been rebuilt with `git-core` installed.  Can you give it another go?
